title: Live Slack and SDK acceptance smoke
description: Blocked: repo has no .env/Slack app credentials. Run spec acceptance #0 (Bolt+Bun Socket Mode event/reply), #6 (real curation fork transcript + cross-thread memory), #8 (real pm2 idle/mid-run restart), and #11 (real Stop button interrupt), then close only with captured evidence.
notes: Permission schema incident fixed. Runtime transcript showed SDK 0.3.201 Zod union requires `updatedInput` for behavior=allow despite sdk.d.ts marking it optional; bridge returned only {behavior:'allow'}, so clicked approvals became tool errors. Approval registry now stores original tool input and returns {behavior:'allow', updatedInput}. Operator requested Bash validation disabled: all Runs now set allowedTools=['Bash(*)', ...channel rules], retaining disallowed Bash(rm -rf:*) speed bump. /Users/jaredsmith/Projects added globally to additionalDirectories for every channel (previous config was only C0BGTU80LAX, so C0BH7C3CE7N stayed blocked). Real SDK proof: Write callback with updatedInput executed once and created expected file; Bash Projects command executed with 0 permission callbacks. 132 tests, tsc, build pass; boot 2026-07-14T02:26:11.792Z.

title: Layer deployment and channel permissions
description:
## Problem
Permission grants are split between channel config and hard-coded process-wide overrides. `src/runs.ts` grants `/Users/jaredsmith/Projects` and `Bash(*)` to every interactive Run, while `src/cron.ts` separately assembles unattended permissions. There is no explicit deployment policy or validated channel-within-deployment composition.

## Decision
Add one root `permissions.json` as the deployment-wide standing-grant ceiling and keep channel grants explicit in `data/channels/<id>/config.json`. Deployment policy authorizes directory roots and Bash command prefixes; a channel must request every directory/command it receives. Omitted or empty channel permission arrays grant nothing. Entries outside the deployment ceiling are configuration errors that refuse the Run or cron fire; never silently intersect or broaden. Directory authorization uses canonical real paths plus containment, not string-prefix or literal-array intersection.

The deployment ceiling governs standing auto-approvals, not an OS sandbox: `additionalDirectories` scopes Claude file tools, and interactive Slack approvals may still authorize one-off requests. Existing DCG and explicit deny rules remain defense in depth. Unattended cron uses the same resolved standing grants with `permissionMode: "dontAsk"`; it never inherits interactive `acceptEdits`.

## Canonical names
- Deployment artifact: `permissions.json`
- Root directory ceiling: `directoryRoots`
- Bash rules at both layers: `commandPrefixes`
- Channel request: nested `permissions.directories` and `permissions.commandPrefixes`
- Runtime value: `ResolvedPermissions`
Avoid `server` (the bot runs locally), `global` (ambiguous), `whitelist` (does not describe precedence), and `repos` (access may target non-repository directories).

## Success Criteria
- One validated deployment policy and one validated channel request compose through a single resolver seam.
- No hard-coded process-wide directory or Bash wildcard remains.
- Unknown channels and omitted permission fields receive no deployment grants.
- Invalid/out-of-ceiling channel grants fail closed with a path-specific/config-specific Slack error.
- Interactive and cron option assembly consume the same resolved policy.
- Tests cover missing/invalid files, path containment and symlinks, command boundary matching, unknown channels, reload timing, interactive prompts, and unattended denial.
- Design spec and sample configuration document exact semantics and migration.

## References
`src/types.ts`, `src/channels.ts`, `src/runs.ts`, `src/cron.ts`, `test/channels.test.ts`, `test/runs.test.ts`, `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-13-slackbot-design.md`
acceptance_criteria: One boot-validated DeploymentPermissionPolicy and one per-run channel request compose through a single resolver seam. Deployment values are a standing-grant ceiling; omitted or empty channel arrays grant nothing. No hard-coded process-wide directory or Bash wildcard remains. Invalid or out-of-ceiling channel grants fail closed with a precise Slack-visible error. Interactive and cron options consume the same resolved policy; cron is deny-only outside grants. Control-plane files (permissions.json, channel configs) cannot be agent-modified to escalate a later run. Focused tests and the design spec cover exact semantics and migration.
notes: Operator direction 2026-07-14: server layer shipped as standing GRANTS shared by all channels, not only a ceiling. permissions.json at repo root ({directories, commandPrefixes}) now merges into every channel's effective repos/allowedCommands via createChannelContextLoader (interactive + cron). Seeded with the union of all existing channel grants. Before implementing p97.1, reconcile the ceiling/request resolver design with this union-grant behavior: server file = grants everyone holds; channel config = channel-specific additions; decide whether a separate ceiling is still wanted.

title: Resolve deployment and channel permission grants
description:
## Target
`src/types.ts`, a focused permission-policy module, and its focused tests. Do not wire SDK callers yet.

## Change
Define `DeploymentPermissionPolicy`, `ChannelPermissionRequest`, and `ResolvedPermissions`. Load root `permissions.json` once at boot through an injected loader. Parse channel `permissions.directories` and `permissions.commandPrefixes` per context load. Expand `~`, require existing directories, canonicalize with `realpath`, and authorize a requested directory only when it equals or is contained by a canonical `directoryRoots` entry using `path.relative`. Validate command prefixes as trimmed, non-empty SDK Bash prefix rules; support explicit `*` only as a deliberate full-command ceiling. Resolve exact normalized channel requests against the deployment ceiling. Omitted/empty channel arrays resolve empty. Duplicate grants collapse while preserving order. Any malformed or out-of-ceiling request throws a precise configuration error; never silently drop or intersect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid nested and descendant directories resolve to canonical paths.
- Sibling-prefix paths and symlinks escaping a root are rejected.
- Explicit symlink paths whose real target remains inside a root resolve safely.
- Command requests require exact normalized membership unless the deployment ceiling contains `*`.
- Missing/unparseable/invalid root policy fails boot; malformed channel requests fail that Run.
- Unknown channel `{}` resolves zero grants.
- Public resolver interface is the only place callers need composition semantics.
- Focused tests prove every behavior above.

## Notes
Use `directoryRoots`, `commandPrefixes`, `permissions.directories`, and `ResolvedPermissions` consistently. This is a standing-grant resolver, not an OS sandbox.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical equal or descendant directories resolve; sibling-prefix and symlink escapes reject. Command requests require exact normalized ceiling membership or explicit deployment wildcard. Omitted or empty channel requests resolve zero grants. Missing, unparseable, or invalid deployment policy fails boot. Malformed or out-of-ceiling channel grants throw a precise error. Focused tests exercise every resolver contract through its public interface.
notes: permissions.json lives at the repo root beside models.json/providers.json; the loader is injected so tests use temp paths. Vocabulary (2026-07-14 name review): the boot-validated type is DeploymentPermissionPolicy; channel side is ChannelPermissionRequest; runtime value is ResolvedPermissions.

title: Migrate channel permission configuration
description:
## Target
`src/channels.ts`, `src/types.ts`, existing `data/channels/*/config.json`, and channel-loader tests. Do not assemble SDK options.

## Change
Replace top-level channel `repos` and `allowedCommands` with one nested request:
```json
{"permissions":{"directories":["~/Projects/foo"],"commandPrefixes":["pm2","bun test"]}}
```
Keep `model` and interactive `permissionMode` at channel level. Inject the deployment policy/resolver into `createChannelContextLoader`; return resolved permissions in `ChannelContext`. Auto-created unknown channel config stays `{}` and therefore receives zero directory/command grants. Migrate every checked-in/live channel config to explicit requests under the deployment ceiling. Clean cutover: no aliases, fallback parsing, or deprecated keys. Unknown legacy keys continue to warn, but migration must remove them from current files. Update prompt repository/access listing to use resolved directories.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing channel behavior is represented explicitly after migration; no channel inherits root grants.
- `{}` and omitted `permissions` yield zero grants.
- Malformed nested values and out-of-ceiling grants refuse the Run with the config path and offending value.
- Relative channel directories resolve relative to the channel directory before canonical ceiling validation; `~` expansion remains supported.
- `permissionMode` remains only `default | acceptEdits` for interactive Runs.
- Channel tests cover migration shape, unknown channels, invalid values, and exact prompt contents.
acceptance_criteria: Every existing channel config is migrated to nested explicit permission requests. Unknown empty-object channels receive zero directory and command grants. Legacy permission keys are removed with no runtime compatibility aliases. Relative and home-relative directory requests resolve before ceiling validation. Channel-loader tests cover migration shape, invalid values, unknown channels, and prompt content.
notes: 2026-07-14: server-wide permissions.json (grant-union semantics) already merges into ChannelContext via the loader; channel configs were left untouched and now partially duplicate server grants. Migration must account for the shipped file: channel configs should keep only channel-specific extras after the layered model lands.

title: Apply resolved permissions to runs and cron
description:
## Target
`src/index.ts`, `src/runs.ts`, `src/cron.ts`, `src/approvals.ts` only as needed, plus focused interactive and cron tests.

## Change
Assemble all SDK directory and Bash standing grants from `ChannelContext.resolvedPermissions`. Remove `PROJECTS_DIR`, interactive `Bash(*)`, and every duplicate permission-composition path. Centralize conversion from a validated `commandPrefix` to the SDK rule `Bash(<prefix>:*)`. Interactive Runs retain channel `permissionMode` and the Slack approval bridge: resolved grants skip prompts; unmatched actions may still request one-off approval. This deployment policy is explicitly a standing-grant ceiling, not a hard OS sandbox. Preserve DCG and explicit deny rules. Cron uses the same resolved directories and command-prefix rules but always sets `permissionMode: "dontAsk"`; unmatched tools are denied without Slack interaction, including Edit/Write even when the channel interactive mode is `acceptEdits`.

Protect control-plane files (`permissions.json` and channel configs) from agent self-escalation: place them outside grantable directories or add an every-tool enforcement boundary that blocks direct, relative, symlinked, and Bash-mediated mutations. The chosen mechanism must cover the actual runtime deployment layout, not only built-in Edit/Write.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Interactive options contain only channel-resolved `additionalDirectories` and Bash rules.
- Empty/unknown channels receive neither hard-coded Projects access nor Bash wildcard approval.
- Two channels with disjoint grants cannot inherit each other's standing grants.
- Interactive unmatched actions still reach Slack approval; resolved actions do not.
- Cron explicitly uses `dontAsk`; resolved Bash prefixes execute, all unmatched tools deny, and `acceptEdits` never leaks into cron.
- Permission/control files cannot be modified by a granted interactive or cron action, including through symlinks or Bash.
- Focused tests cover option assembly, prompt routing, cron denial, and control-plane mutation attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Interactive SDK options contain only resolved channel directories and Bash prefix rules. Empty or unknown channels receive no Projects override or Bash wildcard. Unmatched interactive actions reach Slack approval; standing grants skip it. Cron always uses dontAsk; unmatched tools, Edit, and Write deny. Control-plane files resist direct, relative, symlinked, and Bash-mediated mutation. Focused interactive and cron tests prove these behaviors.
notes: Interim hot-fix landed 2026-07-14 (clarify review): src/cron.ts no longer inherits channel permissionMode - unattended fires hardcode "default" so acceptEdits cannot leak into cron; covered by test/cron.test.ts. This bead still owns the dontAsk migration, resolved-permission option assembly, PROJECTS_DIR/Bash(*) removal, and control-plane protection.

title: Document and verify layered permission policy
description:
## Target
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-13-slackbot-design.md`, a checked-in `permissions.json` example/current policy, `.env.example` only if a path override is introduced, and focused end-to-end permission tests.

## Change
Update the design from channel-only access plus operator hard-codes to the deployment standing-grant ceiling + explicit channel grants. Document configuration examples, exact omission/empty/error semantics, canonical-path containment, command-prefix behavior, boot-time deployment snapshot versus per-run channel reload, interactive one-off Slack escalation, cron deny-only behavior, and control-plane immutability. State clearly that this feature governs standing grants and is not an OS sandbox; Bash and approved interactive actions can exceed SDK file-tool directory scope unless separately constrained. Include migration instructions and remove obsolete `repos`, `allowedCommands`, Projects override, and Bash wildcard descriptions.

Add contract tests spanning the public loader/resolver through interactive and cron option assembly. Test one full migrated channel, an unknown channel, an out-of-ceiling directory, an out-of-ceiling command, a symlink escape, a channel config edit taking effect on the next run/fire, and a deployment policy edit requiring restart.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec, examples, runtime names, errors, and tests use one vocabulary: deployment permission policy, standing-grant ceiling, channel permissions, directoryRoots/directories, commandPrefixes, ResolvedPermissions.
- No documentation claims a hard whitelist or sandbox.
- Every obsolete hard-coded/global behavior is removed from the spec.
- Focused tests pass and typecheck is clean.
- A manual operator smoke records the effective grants for at least two channels and proves they differ.
acceptance_criteria: Spec and examples use the canonical layered-permission vocabulary. Documentation distinguishes standing grants from an OS sandbox and explains one-off Slack escalation. Obsolete repos, allowedCommands, Projects override, and Bash wildcard behavior are removed. End-to-end contract tests pass and typecheck is clean. Manual smoke proves effective grants differ between two channels.
